title: Renaissance Football in Florence - Delicious Italy
highlights:
Florence's football or soccer team may play in pink, yet there is another, older game played every year in Florence. The feast of St.John (Giovanni) the Baptist, patron of Florence, is celebrated by the Florentines with a no-holds-barred Renaissance version of soccer.  ... Each quartieri fields a team of 27 players for a three game sudden death tournament. title: Calcio Storico: the history of the Florentine football
highlights: by some to be the ancestor of the modern game of football, Calcio Storico (also known as 'calcio in costume' or 'calcio in livrea') dates all the way back to Renaissance times. A rough pre-cursor to today's global sport, "Florentine Football" is a rougher affair, often devolving into massive brawls on the sand covered pitch erected every year in June in Piazza Santa Croce. The players during Renaissance times came from the four historic districts into which Florence was divided and where  ...    Croce (blue).
